Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And To Set Aside And Vacate Default Judgement.
Off Calendar Nov-13-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Rena Harel (Petitioner). Owen Clements and Carol Ruwart for City and County of San Francisco.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to service of the summons. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative as follows: 1) Respondent's motion to quash service of summons is granted. Petitioner failed to serve the summons and petition on the proper party, which is the Office of the Mayor or the Clerk of the San Francisco Board of Supervisors. 2) Respondent's motion to set aside and vacate the default judgment is granted. Petitioner served the summons and petition on the San Francisco County Tax Assessor instead of the Office of the Mayor or the Clerk of the San Francisco Board of Supervisors. Thus, service is ineffective and the default judgment is void. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On All Of The Remaining Issues With Respect To The First And Final Account And Compensation
Off Calendar Jul-10-2012 Continued To Aug-08-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Rena Harel. Janet Boessenceker for Randal Anders and multiple Heirs. Daniel Hunter appeared by telephone for Margie Lewis (Beneficiary).

Ms. Rasch stated that the Victim Compensation Board's claim is because a Son of a post deceased Heir was located, that he is in prison, that they have received some 13100 declarations, that they have not received his 13100 declaration and that there is a dispute between the Heir Hunter and the Victim Compensation Board over who has priority. In addition Ms. Rasch stated that they searched for and were unable to find a social number, that the Bank did not have a social security number on the Decedent's accounts and that the City and County of San Francisco's position is that they will not consider an application for exemption of the property taxes without a social security number. Ms. Boessenecker indicated that she did a search as well and was able to find a social security number, and provided the number to Ms. Rasch. Status on all of the remaining issues with respect to the first and final account was continued to August 8, 2012 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Rasch is to submit something with regard to the City and County's position, efforts made in locating a social security number, what was submitted to the City and County, what the City and County's response was, the issues of Victim Compensation, what social security number was used when they approached the City and what happens when there is no social security number. Mr. Hunter will appear by court call.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.
